meaning: n.(单位面积或体积内)生物的数量
examples: In fruit mature period, muskmelon had higher biomass in organic production system, but there was no significant difference on market yield in both systems.
That matters because theory suggests that the maximum sustainable yield that can be cropped from a fishery comes when the biomass of a target species is about 50% of its original levels.
The biomass of Keteleeria has been investigated and analyzed.
Plans for a biomass power station at Southampton docks have been delayed for a second time.
Getting that switchgrass or other biomass to the refinery will be expensive and difficult.
